findings.title: Interprocedural taint: sendFrame() at line 415
findings.description: Tainted argument flows to sink through `sendFrame()`. Call at L415: `sendFrame( new CloseFrameBuilder( code, message ) )`.
findings.suggestion: Review the data flow through helper methods. Ensure input validation at the entry point.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (SHA1) at line 181
findings.description: `getInstance("SHA1")` at L181. SHA1 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak cryptographic hash in `generateFinalKey()`
findings.description: MD5 or SHA-1 used for hashing — vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 via MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256").

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) at line 39
findings.description: `new Random()` used at L39. `java.util.Random` is not cryptographically secure. An attacker can predict its output.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for security-sensitive values (tokens, session IDs, crypto keys). `new SecureRandom()` is the drop-in replacement.
